Each nugget of information about people and the world each gets their own ball in the bingo wheel, and as many times as these nuggets of information, or new nuggets of information, show up in our culture via media, etc., the wheel gets a new ball. When someone spins the wheel and receives a ball, that ball is a randomized bit of information, and its chances of being revealed involve how many times that same type of ball exists in the wheel. There is also someone who monitors the balls in the wheel. Balls including information that is considered unethical by the monitor or statistically proven to be incorrect, are removed from the wheel.

Let's say I give you a job translating random phrases from German to Mandarin.  The only problem is you don't speak either language.  I could give you a bunch of dictionaries and phrasebooks to look everything up in.  Or I could give you thousands and thousands of random German phrases for you to translate with guides or training.  But every few hundred you would have one that has already been translated to Mandarin for you.   The more and more you get one of these the better you will become at translating them.  Eventually, you might get so good that an outside observer would think you spoke both languages.  But you have no idea what any of it means. You don't understand the meanings of any of the words it is just data for you to take from point A to B.   AI is just data going from a to b, question to answer.

I feel like the current focus on using AI to create pseudo-creative work is like the latter. Develop AI to handle mundane tasks so humans have more time to develop creatively. AI is like an almost infinitely large bingo wheel and each ball includes ideas and facts about people and the world. Each nugget of information about people and the world each gets their own ball in the bingo wheel, and as many times as these nuggets of information, or new nuggets of information, show up in our culture via media, etc., the wheel gets a new ball. People who spin the wheel also, sometimes unknowingly, are inserting new balls of information every time they share something. When someone spins the wheel and receives a ball, that ball is a randomized bit of information, and its chances of being revealed involve how many times that same type of ball exists in the wheel. Let's say I give you a job translating random phrases from German to Mandarin.  The only problem is you don't speak either language.  I could give you a bunch of dictionaries and phrasebooks to look everything up in.  Or I could give you thousands and thousands of random German phrases for you to translate with guides or training.  But every few hundred you would have one that has already been translated to Mandarin for you.   The more and more you get one of these the better you will become at translating them.  Eventually, you might get so good that an outside observer would think you spoke both languages.  But you have no idea what any of it means. You don't understand the meanings of any of the words it is just data for you to take from point A to B.   AI is just data going from a to b, question to answer.

I feel like the current focus on using AI to create pseudo-creative work is like the latter. Develop AI to handle mundane tasks so humans have more time to develop creatively. AI is like an almost infinitely large bingo wheel and each ball includes ideas and facts about people and the world. Each nugget of information about people and the world each gets their own ball in the bingo wheel, and as many times as these nuggets of information, or new nuggets of information, show up in our culture via media, etc., the wheel gets a new ball. People who spin the wheel also, sometimes unknowingly, are inserting new balls of information every time they share something. When someone spins the wheel and receives a ball, that ball is a randomized bit of information, and its chances of being revealed involve how many times that same type of ball exists in the wheel. There is also someone who monitors the balls in the wheel. Balls including information that is considered unethical by the monitor or statistically proven to be incorrect, are removed from the wheel.

So, if you asked an AI if it thought sunsets or sunrises were prettier, it would probably tell you, based on the fact 68% of people who asked this before think sunsets are prettier, that sunsets are prettier. Let's say I give you a job translating random phrases from German to Mandarin.  The only problem is you don't speak either language.  I could give you a bunch of dictionaries and phrasebooks to look everything up in.  Or I could give you thousands and thousands of random German phrases for you to translate with guides or training.  But every few hundred you would have one that has already been translated to Mandarin for you.   The more and more you get one of these the better you will become at translating them.  Eventually, you might get so good that an outside observer would think you spoke both languages.  But you have no idea what any of it means. You don't understand the meanings of any of the words it is just data for you to take from point A to B.   AI is just data going from a to b, question to answer.

To me, AI seems sort of like a hive, a collective where it takes from individual workers (like worker bees) to create something larger. This could be used for good -- after all, a hive provides necessary shelter for bees and allows the swarm to sustain itself -- but if it's used in the wrong ways, it can be more of a detriment than a benefit (imagine a poorly build hive full of holes that lets in the winter chill). I feel like the current focus on using AI to create pseudo-creative work is like the latter. Develop AI to handle mundane tasks so humans have more time to develop creatively. Don't use AI to write a poorly-worded, nonsensical novel -- use AI to, say, clean the house and do other chores, and put the time you save toward writing a GOOD novel yourself.

AI is like an almost infinitely large bingo wheel and each ball includes ideas and facts about people and the world. Each nugget of information about people and the world each gets their own ball in the bingo wheel, and as many times as these nuggets of information, or new nuggets of information, show up in our culture via media, etc., the wheel gets a new ball. People who spin the wheel also, sometimes unknowingly, are inserting new balls of information every time they share something. When someone spins the wheel and receives a ball, that ball is a randomized bit of information, and its chances of being revealed involve how many times that same type of ball exists in the wheel. There is also someone who monitors the balls in the wheel. Balls including information that is considered unethical by the monitor or statistically proven to be incorrect, are removed from the wheel.

So, if you asked an AI if it thought sunsets or sunrises were prettier, it would probably tell you, based on the fact 68% of people who asked this before think sunsets are prettier, that sunsets are prettier. Let's say I give you a job translating random phrases from German to Mandarin.  The only problem is you don't speak either language.  I could give you a bunch of dictionaries and phrasebooks to look everything up in.  Or I could give you thousands and thousands of random German phrases for you to translate with guides or training.  But every few hundred you would have one that has already been translated to Mandarin for you.   The more and more you get one of these the better you will become at translating them.  Eventually, you might get so good that an outside observer would think you spoke both languages.  But you have no idea what any of it means. You don't understand the meanings of any of the words it is just data for you to take from point A to B.   AI is just data going from a to b, question to answer.
